summary: Game-cheat dropper with Windows persistence, HWID exfiltration, and obfuscated payload
details: Package masquerades as a 'Sensivity Control Panel' but ships a game-cheat loader. On module load, launcher.js executes a PowerShell command that writes an HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run entry named 'OneDriveUpdate' (impersonating Microsoft OneDrive) pointing to a bundled OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs that silently relaunches `node launcher.js` hidden at every user logon — establishing persistence on the installer's Windows account. launcher.js then reads server.obf.js and passes its bytes to eval(); server.obf.js is heavily packed with obfuscator.io (397-element rotated RC4 string array, _0xNNNN identifiers) and at runtime collects a host fingerprint by reading HKLM Cryptography MachineGuid, os.hostname(), and the C: volume serial via PowerShell Get-Volume, SHA-256 hashes them, and POSTs the hash plus license key/nonce to a hardcoded remote license endpoint. A 6.8MB native Windows PE addon `sens.node` is loaded as `forte` and exposes setConfig/start/stop; embedded strings ('Teleport','LeftFoot','LeftHand','Head','Settings') indicate skeletal-bone targeting (aimbot/teleport game-cheat). Activation is gated on detecting a Chrome window whose title contains 'YouTube' — an evasion designed to hide the cheat from anti-cheat overlays and streamers. Any developer who installs and loads this package receives silent autostart persistence under a Microsoft-impersonating registry name, has their machine fingerprinted to a third-party server, and runs an opaque native binary.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
